ideas_used: team spec separate from runtime state
role/category based dispatch instead of model picking
default delegation bias except for super-simple local work
dependency graph and parallel execution waves for plans

notes: Check global [agents] settings before broad fan-out.
Keep max_depth at the default unless recursive delegation is explicitly requested.
Use spawn_agents_on_csv for large uniform batches with one narrow task per row.

smartest_deep.description: Hard planning, ambiguity analysis, and plan gates.

smartest_review.description: Independent review where correctness matters more than latency.

fast_scan.description: Read-heavy scans, exploration, and lightweight fan-out.

intent_gate.answer: Explanation, design opinion, or conceptual response without requested edits.
intent_gate.investigate: Look into, check, compare, or diagnose without explicit fix authorization.
intent_gate.implement: Build, create, add, write, modify, or change code or artifacts.
intent_gate.fix: Broken behavior, error message, failing test, or regression.
intent_gate.review: Code review, plan review, QA review, or validation request.
intent_gate.plan: Planning, breakdown, or execution strategy request.
intent_gate.terminal: Simple shell command or status check only.

domains.visual: UI, CSS, layout, animation, frontend components, visual polish.
domains.game: Gameplay, physics, collision, scoring, levels, input loops.
domains.logic: Algorithms, architecture, state machines, business logic.
domains.data: SQL, spreadsheets, data transforms, schemas, reports.
domains.infra: CI, deployment, scripts, auth, permissions, services.
domains.general: None of the above or not yet clear.

allow_main_thread_only_when_all_hold: Known file, command, or explanation target.
No meaningful product, design, or architecture judgment.
Roughly less than 10 lines of obvious change if editing.
self_gate.single_file_note: One requested output file limits write scope; it does not make a complex app or game simple.

levels.low: Simple questions, tiny commands, copy edits, style tweaks, and very small isolated bug fixes.
levels.medium: Bounded single features, straightforward single-file UI, simple arcade mechanics, and small CRUD flows.
levels.high: Complete user-facing apps or games, multiple interacting states, physics, collision, drag interactions, timers, scoring, levels, generated assets, nontrivial validation, or more than one meaningful subsystem.
levels.very_high: Ambiguous architecture, security-sensitive changes, destructive operations, large refactors, migrations, cross-service work, or complex concurrency/data-loss risk.

game_rules: Classify games by mechanics rather than file count.
Basic snake, pong, clicker, memory, and tic-tac-toe can remain small when the user asks for a quick version.
New playable games with several mechanics are at least complex.
Physics or collision games are complex by default.
Angry Birds-style tasks are complex because they imply slingshot aiming, projectile motion, collision response, destructible or moving targets, scoring, level state, and restart/win/lose flow.
prompt_complexity.authorization: An explicit request to use Codex Team Router for a coding task authorizes complexity-based delegation. Explicit subagent, delegate, parallel, or planner/executor/reviewer wording also authorizes spawning. If the skill was selected only implicitly and tool policy requires explicit delegation authorization, ask before spawning.

trivial.description: Simple questions, tiny edits, simple terminal commands, or explanation-only work.
trivial.verification: Answer directly or run the simple command; do not create team-router overhead.

small.description: Low prompt complexity where the self gate passes.

standard.description: Medium prompt complexity: bounded single features, straightforward single-file UI, simple arcade mechanics, and small CRUD flows.
standard.verification: Planner defines the bounded approach, executor implements, reviewer gates, main thread verifies.

parallel_read.description: Read-heavy exploration, large-file review, log triage, documentation slicing, or uniform scan work.
parallel_read.verification: Main thread synthesizes returned summaries and checks cited evidence.

complex.description: High prompt complexity: multi-step implementation, complete user-facing app or game, complex single-file interactive artifact, multi-file work, physics/collision gameplay, product/design judgment, nontrivial validation, or likely >50 lines of coherent new work.
complex.verification: Planner defines scope, executor implements, reviewer gates, main thread validates.

high_risk.description: Ambiguous, security-sensitive, destructive, cross-boundary, architecture-heavy, or high-blast-radius work.
high_risk.verification: Run analysis and plan gates before implementation; require independent review and validation evidence before completion.

responsibilities: recommend safe assumptions or user decisions
gate the plan with okay, iterate, or reject
edit files directly within owned scope
report changed paths and open issues
call out missing validation or evidence
run independent validation when main-thread verification is expensive

hard_reject.reason: The main thread is always the coordinator in Codex App native mode.
Executors must have an explicit owned file or directory scope.
